name: dmatrix_squadrack
_comment: d-Matrix SquadRack full-rack reference, modeled at chiplet granularity (8 servers × 8 cards × 8 chiplets = 512 chiplets). Per-chiplet device spec same as `dmatrix.server`. peak_flops_TF=150 stores FP16-equivalent dense per chiplet under the framework convention (FP16 baseline; linear byte scaling: MXINT8 = 300, MXINT4 = 600 derived). Caveat: published MXINT4 is 1200 TF/chiplet — framework under-states d-Matrix INT4/FP4 by 2× due to block-sparse acceleration not captured by linear byte scaling. Fabric chain decomposes the rack hierarchy into 5 tiers (cumulative reach 4 → 16 → 32 → 64 → 512): (1) `package_d2d` — 2×2 full mesh of 4 chiplets within a single Corsair package via D2D links, 128 GB/s, 0.115 μs (whitepaper Fig 6). (2) `dmx_bridge` — 4 packages joined across 2 cards via the DMX Bridge (= 16-chiplet 'high-speed scale-up unit' per d-Matrix marketing), 64 GB/s, 0.4 μs (whitepaper Fig 9). (3a) `pcie5-intra-hemisphere` — 2 sets of 2-card pairs (= 32 chiplets) within one host PCIe-switch hemisphere, 64 GB/s, 0.6 μs. (3b) `pcie5-inter-hemisphere` — 2 hemispheres joined across the host-CPU PCIe switches (= 64-chiplet full server), 64 GB/s, 0.6 μs. (4) `ethernet_rack` — 8 servers per rack via 4 JetStream NICs per server (1 NIC per 16-chiplet group, brief Sept 2025 Fig 2). Each JetStream is 400 Gbps = 50 GB/s single-direction (PCIe Gen5 x16 host, overprovisioned vs the 50 GB/s wire). Per-port BW = 50 GB/s models one JetStream egress per server (conservative collective-traffic model — additional NICs are available for non-collective host I/O or wider striping but not assumed). 2.0 μs transparent-NIC latency (Hot Chips 2025). INC structurally absent on the entire chain (no SHARP-class hardware). Inter-rack scale-out fabric not modeled. Sources: d-Matrix Corsair Technical Whitepaper (Nov 2024), JetStream Product Brief (Sept 2025), Hot Chips 2025 coverage.
